label: SCADA Mimic — Tank & Pump
info:
## Tank & Pump Mimic Example

Demonstrates the `ui-scada-mimic` node with declarative tag bindings.

### What it shows
- **Tank TK-01**: level fill animation (bottom-up), numeric level display
- **Pump P-101**: run-state color change (gray → blue), status text (STOPPED/RUNNING), live amps
- **Click interaction**: clicking the pump circle emits a control intent (topic: `open-faceplate`) — wire this to a `ui-scada-faceplate` node to open the motor faceplate

### How the data flows
```
Inject (2 s) → Tag simulator → ui-scada-mimic → Debug (control intents)
```
The `inject` + `function` (Tag simulator) nodes produce a normalized tag map with quality metadata every 2 seconds. In production, replace them with your OPC UA / Modbus / MQTT / Sparkplug source node.

### Tag map format
```json
{
  "TK01.level": { "value": 67.2, "quality": "good", "units": "%" },
  "P101.run":   { "value": true,  "quality": "good" },
  "P101.amps":  { "value": 11.4,  "quality": "good", "units": "A" }
}
```

### Binding DSL highlights
- `level` target: animates a `<rect>` height from the bottom up (tank fill)
- `style` target + `valueMap`: switches fill color between running/stopped states
- `text` target + `format`: displays numeric value with unit suffix
- `events[]`: wires element click to an output message

name: Tank + Pump
svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 300">
  <rect width="400" height="300" fill="var(--hmi-bg-display,#f5f5f5)"/>

  <!-- Tank shell -->
  <rect id="TK01_body" x="40" y="44" width="100" height="152" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="#616161" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- Tank fill — level binding grows this rect from bottom up -->
  <rect id="TK01_fill" x="44" y="44" width="92" height="152" fill="#5c85d6" opacity="0.6"/>
  <!-- Tank label (stroke=none prevents glyph bleed from parent g stroke) -->
  <text x="90" y="218" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#424242" stroke="none">TK-01</text>
  <!-- Numeric level display -->
  <text id="TK01_level_text" x="90" y="232" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="10" fill="#212121" stroke="none">-- %</text>

  <!-- Process pipe from tank to pump -->
  <line x1="140" y1="170" x2="210" y2="155" stroke="#757575" stroke-width="5"/>

  <!-- Pump body — fill changes with run state -->
  <circle id="P101_body" cx="240" cy="140" r="32" fill="#9e9e9e" stroke="#616161" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- Pump impeller symbol -->
  <polygon id="P101_arrow" points="240,120 260,152 220,152" fill="#616161"/>
  <!-- Equipment tag label -->
  <text x="240" y="188" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="11" font-weight="bold" fill="#424242" stroke="none">P-101</text>
  <!-- Run-state status text -->
  <text id="P101_status" x="240" y="201" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#616161" stroke="none">STOPPED</text>
  <!-- Live amps display -->
  <text id="P101_amps" x="240" y="213" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-size="9" fill="#424242" stroke="none">-- A</text>

  <!-- Transparent click target over pump — wired in events[] to emit open-faceplate -->
  <circle id="P101_click" cx="240" cy="140" r="34" fill="transparent" style="cursor:pointer"/>
</svg>

name: Tag simulator
Control intents
info:
Shows messages emitted when an operator clicks an interactive SVG element.
Click the pump circle on the dashboard to see a message here:
{ topic: 'open-faceplate', payload: { equip: 'P101', type: 'motor' } }

In production, wire this to a ui-scada-faceplate node or to your protocol write node.
